ideal_must_detect.matched_text: BKAT is not listed on any exchange
T r a d i n g   b l a c k o u t   w i n d o w   r e m i n d e r s   t o   b e   a u t o ‑ n o t i f i e d
bridge risk may trigger intangible impairment in post‑closing purchase accounting
LRHI to acquire 80% of Target’s outstanding shares
Project Talangka Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of BitKat Remit Corp. (PH private) by Luneta Renewables Holdings, Inc. (PH private).

Transaction: LRHI to acquire 80% of Target’s outstanding shares; consideration in Philippine Pesos; intended sustainability-linked financing; filings to be made under the Philippine Securities Regulation Code with the Securities and Listings Bureau (SLB‑Ph) and, if required, the Mabini Stock Exchange (MSE), subject to confidentiality controls.

Digital assets/crypto: Target operates the BKAT token and a cross‑chain bridge; pre‑closing covenants require disabling mint functions, wind‑down of non‑KYC wallets, and no announcement of token features; BKAT is not listed on any exchange and may be converted or burned at Buyer’s option; bridge risk may trigger intangible impairment in post‑closing purchase accounting.

Cyber and data: Due diligence notes a 12 May 2026 credential‑stuffing incident (non‑public); remediation underway with BugShield Pro EDR deployment and third‑party attestations pending; closing conditional on clean penetration test and evidence of customer notification templates per privacy policy.

ESG/climate: Condition precedent for climate‑risk assessment of data center energy mix and renewable PUE; Seller to source REC equivalents for 24 months; covenants for GHG scope 2 intensity baseline and quarterly emissions reporting to support green financing.

must_detect.matched_text:
Project Talangka Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of BitKat Remit Corp. (PH private) by Luneta Renewables Holdings, Inc. (PH private).
Primary contacts: Atty. Marisela Quinto (Buyer Counsel) marisela.quinto@lrhi.ph; Mr. Elvin R. Dalisay (Seller CFO) elvin.dalisay@bitkatremit.ph; office: Unit 1902, Larawan Center, Barangay Liwayway, San Pedro Norte, Metro Balintawak 1245 (fictional).
Transaction: LRHI to acquire 80% of Target’s outstanding shares; consideration in Philippine Pesos; intended sustainability-linked financing; filings to be made under the Philippine Securities Regulation Code with the Securities and Listings Bureau (SLB‑Ph) and, if required, the Mabini Stock Exchange (MSE), subject to confidentiality controls.
Consideration and escrow: PHP 1,250,000,000; 10% escrow at IslaTrust Cooperative acct 00-9x-44-113 for 18 months; completion accounts with customary net debt/working capital adjustments; DST and local transfer levies borne 50/50.
Digital assets/crypto: Target operates the BKAT token and a cross‑chain bridge; pre‑closing covenants require disabling mint functions, wind‑down of non‑KYC wallets, and no announcement of token features; BKAT is not listed on any exchange and may be converted or burned at Buyer’s option; bridge risk may trigger intangible impairment in post‑closing purchase accounting.
Cyber and data: Due diligence notes a 12 May 2026 credential‑stuffing incident (non‑public); remediation underway with BugShield Pro EDR deployment and third‑party attestations pending; closing conditional on clean penetration test and evidence of customer notification templates per privacy policy.
ESG/climate: Condition precedent for climate‑risk assessment of data center energy mix and renewable PUE; Seller to source REC equivalents for 24 months; covenants for GHG scope 2 intensity baseline and quarterly emissions reporting to support green financing.
Commercial terms: Earn‑out up to PHP 250,000,000 over 24 months if monthly active wallets exceed 500,000 and net take rate ≥ 1.2%; break fee PHP 25,000,000; non‑compete and non‑solicit for 36 months; warranties on wallet balances, private‑key management, and token treasury.
Insider list and blackout: Insider list to be maintained per SRC best practice; trading blackout for LRHI insiders from signing until MSE disclosure; information labeled “confidential nonpublic”; no tipping or selective disclosure to third parties, partners, or influencers.
Information barriers: LRHI Capital Markets and Sales to be ring‑fenced; only deal team access to “Project Talangka” mailbox at dealroom://mse.local/r/9f-a2; audit trail and read‑receipt controls enabled; T r a d i n g   b l a c k o u t   w i n d o w   r e m i n d e r s   t o   b e   a u t o ‑ n o t i f i e d.
Educational/marketing (benign): The retail app user guide uses the words crypto wallet and token bridge in a training screenshot about password hygiene only and does not describe pricing, issuance, or investment features.
HR/tax/ops: Key employee retention pool PHP 60,000,000; no change‑in‑control tax gross‑up; DST and withholdings handled per Pagasa Revenue Authority circulars (fictional); standard PH labor code compliance; migration plan for payroll wallets to bank rails by T+45.
Negative/precision bait: For illustration only, this draft expressly does not include nor trigger any material adverse change concept and cites public‑source climate FAQs (e.g., “Kamalig University open notes”) without reliance on unpublished data.
Disclosure controls: Announcements to MSE/SLB‑Ph and company website only upon clearance; until then, treat all terms as MNPI; management Q&A to be recorded to avoid selective disclosure; url https://docs.lrhi.ph/talangka?uid=AB12-xyZZ-9900.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
